metadata.scheme_name: NDRI Institute Scholarship For M.Sc. And Ph.d
metadata.state: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

raw_data.details: The "NDRI Institute Scholarship for M. Sc. and Ph. D. Programmes" is implemented by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare to provide financial assistance to Indian students admitted to Master's and Doctoral programmes at the National Dairy Research Institute (NDRI). The scholarship is available only to students who do not receive any other financial assistance or scholarship from any source. Financial support is provided in the form of monthly scholarships during the prescribed duration of the academic programme. Continuation of the scholarship is subject to academic performance, course registration requirements, submission of progress reports, and compliance with institutional rules and regulations. No extension of scholarship is granted beyond the prescribed period.
raw_data.benefits:
ProgrammeInstitute Scholarships DetailsMaster's degree₹7,560 P. M. for two years + ₹,6000 per annum as contingencyPh. D.
₹25,000 P. M. for first two years, ₹28,000 during third year, and ₹10,000 per annum as contingency. Ph. D. (In-service)₹3,000 P. M. for three years and ₹10,000 per annum as contingency
raw_data.eligibility: “Institute Scholarship” is offered to all students of Indian Nationality admitted to Master’s and Doctoral programmes at NDRI to provide them with financial assistance as per the rules and conditions in this document. Scholarships are offered to only those who do not hold any other financial Assistance/ Scholarship. As and when a student receives or accepts financial assistance from any other source, the “Institute Scholarship” will be terminated. The award of a scholarship will be made on receipt of the following certificates:Acceptance of the award in the prescribed proforma. An undertaking to follow the rules and regulations as per the conditions governing the Scholarship in the prescribed proforma.
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 1: Obtain admission to the eligible Master's or Doctoral programme at NDRI.
Step 2: Submit the [Acceptance of the Award](https://ndri.res.in//sites/default/files/2025-06/UNDERTAKING_Conditions-of-Award.pdf) in the prescribed format.
Step 3: Submit the Undertaking to comply with scholarship rules and regulations.
Step 4: Complete registration for all prescribed courses in each semester.
Step 5: Submit periodic Progress Reports as prescribed.
Step 6: Maintain the required academic performance for continuation and renewal of the scholarship.
raw_data.documents_required: Admission Proof to Master's or Doctoral Programme at NDRIProof of Indian NationalityAcceptance of Award Form (Annexure-I)Undertaking for Compliance with Scholarship Rules (Annexure-II)Semester Registration RecordsProgress Report (Annexure-IV)Academic Performance Records/Mark SheetsEmployment Certificate (For In-Service Ph. D. Students, If Applicable).

Programmes" is implemented by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare to provide financial assistance to Indian students admitted to Master's and Doctoral programmes at the National Dairy Research Institute (NDRI). The scholarship is available only to students who do not receive any other financial assistance or scholarship from any source. Financial support is provided in the form of monthly scholarships during the prescribed duration of the academic programme. M. for three years and ₹10,000 per annum as contingencyEligibility“Institute Scholarship” is offered to all students of Indian Nationality admitted to Master’s and Doctoral programmes at NDRI to provide them with financial assistance as per the rules and conditions in this document. Scholarships are offered to only those who do not hold any other financial Assistance/ Scholarship. As and when a student receives or accepts financial assistance from any other source, the “Institute Scholarship” will be terminated. The award of a scholarship will be made on receipt of the following certificates:Acceptance of the award in the prescribed proforma. An undertaking to follow the rules and regulations as per the conditions governing the Scholarship in the prescribed proforma. Application ProcessOnlineStep 1: Obtain admission to the eligible Master's or Doctoral programme at NDRI. Step 2: Submit the [Acceptance of the Award](https://ndri.res.in//sites/default/files/2025-06/UNDERTAKING_Conditions-of-Award.pdf) in the prescribed format. Step 3: Submit the Undertaking to comply with scholarship rules and regulations. Step 4: Complete registration for all prescribed courses in each semester. Step 5: Submit periodic Progress Reports as prescribed. Step 6: Maintain the required academic performance for continuation and renewal of the scholarship. Documents RequiredAdmission Proof to Master's or Doctoral Programme at NDRIProof of Indian NationalityAcceptance of Award Form (Annexure-I)Undertaking for Compliance with Scholarship Rules (Annexure-II)Semester Registration RecordsProgress Report (Annexure-IV)Academic Performance Records/Mark SheetsEmployment Certificate (For In-Service Ph. D. Students, If Applicable). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the objective of the NDRI Institute Scholarship? The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Indian students pursuing Master's and Doctoral programmes at NDRI.

Who can apply for this scholarship? Indian students admitted to eligible Master's or Doctoral programmes at NDRI can apply for the scholarship. Can a student receive this scholarship along with another scholarship? No, a student receiving any other financial assistance or scholarship is not eligible for this scholarship.

How much scholarship is provided to Master's students? Eligible Master's students receive ₹7,560 per month.

How much scholarship is provided to Doctoral students? Eligible Doctoral students receive ₹25,000 per month during the first 2 years and ₹28,000 per month during the 3rd year. What is the scholarship amount for in-service Ph. D. students? Eligible in-service Ph. D. students receive ₹3,000 per month subject to prescribed conditions. When does the scholarship begin? The scholarship begins from the date of joining the programme. What is the duration of the scholarship for Master's students? The scholarship continues until thesis submission or completion of the prescribed scholarship period, whichever is earlier. What is the duration of the scholarship for Doctoral students? The scholarship continues until thesis submission or completion of the prescribed scholarship period, whichever is earlier. Is extension of the scholarship allowed? No, extension of the scholarship is not granted under any circumstances. What academic performance is required for continuation of the scholarship? The student must obtain at least 65% marks or 6. 5 Grade Point Average out of 10. 0 in aggregate. What happens if a student fails to achieve the required academic standard? The student may improve performance through supplementary or semester-end examinations and become eligible for renewal after meeting the required standard. Is submission of a Progress Report mandatory? Yes, submission of the prescribed Progress Report is mandatory for release of the scholarship. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://ndri.res.in/scholarships-fellowships)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: What is the objective of the NDRI Institute Scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Indian students pursuing Master's and Doctoral programmes at NDRI.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Indian students admitted to eligible Master's or Doctoral programmes at NDRI can apply for the scholarship.

faqs.question: Can a student receive this scholarship along with another scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, a student receiving any other financial assistance or scholarship is not eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: How much scholarship is provided to Master's students?
faqs.answer: Eligible Master's students receive ₹7,560 per month.

faqs.question: How much scholarship is provided to Doctoral students?
faqs.answer: Eligible Doctoral students receive ₹25,000 per month during the first 2 years and ₹28,000 per month during the 3rd year.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for in-service Ph. D. students?
faqs.answer: Eligible in-service Ph. D. students receive ₹3,000 per month subject to prescribed conditions.

faqs.question: When does the scholarship begin?
faqs.answer: The scholarship begins from the date of joining the programme.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scholarship for Master's students?
faqs.answer: The scholarship continues until thesis submission or completion of the prescribed scholarship period, whichever is earlier.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scholarship for Doctoral students?
faqs.answer: The scholarship continues until thesis submission or completion of the prescribed scholarship period, whichever is earlier.

faqs.question: Is extension of the scholarship allowed?
faqs.answer: No, extension of the scholarship is not granted under any circumstances.

faqs.question: What academic performance is required for continuation of the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The student must obtain at least 65% marks or 6. 5 Grade Point Average out of 10. 0 in aggregate.

faqs.question: What happens if a student fails to achieve the required academic standard?
faqs.answer: The student may improve performance through supplementary or semester-end examinations and become eligible for renewal after meeting the required standard.

faqs.question: Is submission of a Progress Report mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, submission of the prescribed Progress Report is mandatory for release of the scholarship.
